title: Tracery Test Suite
description: All core Tracery features tested in one grammar: plain text, push/pop variables, tag-embedded actions, recursive push, complex pronoun/occupation grammar, and modifiers — based on the original Kate Compton test files.

plaintext_long: Emma Woodhouse, handsome, clever, and rich, with a comfortable home and happy disposition, seemed to unite some of the best blessings of existence.
t_push:
Push Variable

Bracket notation sets a named variable, usable anywhere in the same rule:

[pet:#animal#]You have a #pet#. Your #pet# is #mood#.
t_pushpop:
Push / POP Stack

Push overwrites, POP restores — a basic variable stack:

[pet:#animal#]Outer pet: #pet#. [pet:#animal#]Inner pet: #pet#. [pet:POP]Restored: #pet#.
t_tagaction:
Pre-Action Inside Tag

Embed a push inside a tag expression — the push is scoped to that expansion only:

#[pet:#animal#]nonrecursiveStory#
t_recursive:
Recursive Push

Pet is set, a nested story swaps it, then POP restores the original:

[pet:#animal#]#recursiveStory#
t_complex:
Complex Grammar (Pronouns + Occupation)

Sets pronouns and occupation at origin, then generates a story:

#origin_complex#
recursiveStory: The #pet# opened a book about[pet:#mood# #animal#] #pet.a#. #story_r#[pet:POP] The #pet# closed the book.
name: Cheri
Fox
Morgana
Jedoo
Brick
Shadow
Krox
heroStory: #hero.capitalize# was a great #occupation#, and this song tells of #heroTheir# adventure. #hero.capitalize# #didStuff#, then #heroThey# #didStuff#, then #heroThey# went home to read a book.
grammar._cssStyles: .ts{font-family:sans-serif;padding:1rem;max-width:65ch;color:#1a1a2e} h1{font-size:1.4rem;border-bottom:3px solid #6366f1;padding-bottom:.4rem;color:#4338ca} h2{font-size:1rem;margin:0 0 .4rem;color:#6366f1} section{background:#f8f8ff;border:1px solid #e0e0f0;border-radius:8px;padding:.8rem 1rem;margin-bottom:.8rem} .label{font-size:.85rem;color:#555;margin:.2rem 0 .4rem} .r{margin:.3rem 0;line-height:1.6} .src{font-size:.85rem} .out{background:#fff;border-left:3px solid #6366f1;padding:.3rem .6rem;border-radius:0 4px 4px 0} code{background:#eef;padding:1px 5px;border-radius:3px;font-size:.88em}

title: Symbols & Rules
description: Core Tracery: define symbols and reference them with #symbol# to build varied sentences.

title: Modifiers
description: Use .capitalize, .s (plural) and .ed after a symbol to transform its output automatically.

title: Set Variables
description: Use [name:#symbol#] inside a # expression to set a variable and reuse it consistently.

story:
The Tale of #hero#

#hero# had a pet #heroPet# named #heroPet.capitalize#-Too.

One day, #hero# and the #heroPet# ventured into the #place#.

Nobody believed #hero# when they returned — but the #heroPet# knew the truth.
name: Ash
Ember
River
Sage
Quinn
Wren

title: Nested Symbols
description: Symbols can reference other symbols to build complex, layered outputs from simple rules.

headline: Local #creature.capitalize# #verb.ed# in #place#
#place.capitalize# Shocked by #adj.capitalize# #creature.capitalize#
#adj.capitalize# #creature.capitalize# to Attend #event#
lead: Officials say the #creature# appeared on #day# without warning.
The #adj# #creature# was first spotted near the old #place#.
follow: No comment has been issued by the local #who#.
#who.capitalize# is expected to hold a press conference on #day#.
Further updates are expected from the #who# by end of week.

title: Text Decoration
description: Bold, italic, underline, strikethrough and coloured text using HTML tags and inline CSS.

headline: Today's Story
Breaking News
A Short Tale
sentence: She had a #adj# idea that changed everything.
more: Her notes were <mark>#adj#</mark> and full of <u>underlines</u>.
The sign read: #adj# Zone — Proceed With Care.

title: Text Alignment
description: Left, centre, and right text alignment using CSS text-align.

title: Headers & Links
description: HTML heading levels h1-h3, paragraphs, and anchor links.

title: The Great Discovery
An Unexpected Journey
Secrets of the Deep
subtitle: Chapter One: The Beginning
Part One: Setting Off
Introduction: What Lies Ahead
body: It all started on a #adj# afternoon when nobody expected anything unusual.
The explorer opened the door and found something #adj# inside.
Years of waiting finally paid off — the answer was <u>#adj#</u> simple.

title: Images
description: Display images with custom width, height, border-radius and alignment.

caption: A Quiet Moment
Into the Wild
Urban Life
The Horizon
desc: Look closer — there is always more to see.

title: Colors & Backgrounds
description: Change the page background, text colours and highlights using CSS class names.

title: A Colourful World
Design With Style
Make It Pop
body: #phrase# — colour makes all the difference.
Try bold colour to emphasise your ideas.

title: Float Layout
description: Float elements left and right to create simple two-column layouts.

title: Two Column Layout
Side by Side
Float Example
phrase: The quick fox jumped over the fence.
Always check both sides of the argument.
Details matter more than you think.

title: Tables
description: Basic HTML tables with headers, rows and CSS styling.

title: High Scores
Character Stats
Creature Catalogue
Mission Log
name: Ash
Blaze
Cedar
Dusk
Ember
Frost
grammar._cssStyles: .wrap{font-family:sans-serif;padding:1rem} h2{margin-bottom:.6rem} .tbl{border-collapse:collapse;width:100%} .tbl th{background:rgb(58,58,106);color:white;padding:8px 12px;text-align:left} .tbl td{padding:8px 12px;border-bottom:1px solid rgb(221,221,221)} .tbl tr:nth-child(even) td{background:rgb(245,245,245)}

title: Scrolling Navigation
description: Use scrollto: followed by a CSS selector to create jump-links within the generated document. Great for long stories or encyclopedias. Notice the scroll-margin-top CSS so titles aren't trapped under the sticky nav!

origin:
Contents: Chap 1 | Chap 2 | Footer

Chapter 1

This is a long section. #content#

Chapter 2

This is the second section. #content#

The End ↑ Top
content: Far far away, behind the word mountains, far from the countries Vokalia and Consonantia.
Separated they live in Bookmarksgrove right at the coast of the Semantics, a large language ocean.
A small river named Duden flows by their place and supplies it with the necessary regelialia.

title: Day & Night (Variables)
description: Use [var:#symbol#] to lock a time-of-day theme so creatures, light, and mood are always consistent within each generated story.

grammar.scene:
A #time# in the #place#

The #skyWord# light lay across the #place#. The air was #ambiance#.

A #animalPool# moved through the undergrowth. #moment#

#close#
moment: For a long moment, nothing else moved.
It paused, then continued on its way.
The #place# felt like it was holding its breath.
close: By the time I looked again, it was gone.
The #time# settled around it like a cloak.
That is how I remember it: the #skyWord# light, the silence, and the #animalPool#.

title: Day & Night (Structure)
description: A second approach: separate top-level symbols (dayStory, nightStory) each with their own themed word pools — no variables needed.

grammar.dayStory:
☀ A #dayAdj# #dayTime#

The #dayWeather# sky stretched wide. A #dayAnimal# crossed the #dayPlace#.

#dayMoment#
grammar.nightStory:
🌙 A #nightAdj# #nightTime#

The #nightWeather# sky was deep and wide. A #nightAnimal# moved through the #nightPlace#.

#nightMoment#
dayMoment: The warmth made everything slow and easy.
A distant lark sang once, then was quiet.
Dust drifted in the sunlit air.
nightMoment: Nothing moved for a long while.

title: Four Seasons
description: Each season symbol draws only from season-appropriate imagery — a structural approach to thematic coherence without any variables.

grammar.spring:
🌸 Spring

#springOpening# A #springCreature# appeared among the #springPlant#.

#springClose#
grammar.summer:
☀ Summer

#summerOpening# A #summerCreature# rested in the shade of the #summerPlant#.

#summerClose#
grammar.autumn:
🍂 Autumn

#autumnOpening# A #autumnCreature# moved beneath the #autumnPlant#.

#autumnClose#
grammar.winter:
❄ Winter

#winterOpening# A #winterCreature# crossed the #winterPlace#.

#winterClose#
springOpening: The air smelled of rain and new growth.
springClose: The world felt new and full of promise.
Somewhere, something small was waking up.
summerOpening: The heat pressed down like a warm hand.
autumnOpening: The smell of woodsmoke drifted through the air.
Leaves turned and dropped one by one.
autumnClose: Something was ending, quietly and without fuss.
The light came low and golden.
winterClose: The cold made everything sharp and clear.
grammar._cssStyles: .s{font-family:Georgia,serif;padding:1.4rem;max-width:54ch;line-height:1.85;border-radius:8px;margin:.5rem} h2{font-size:1.2rem;margin-bottom:.5rem} p{margin:.4rem 0} .spring{background:rgb(240,255,245);color:rgb(30,60,40)} .spring h2{color:rgb(60,140,80)} .summer{background:rgb(255,251,220);color:rgb(60,50,20)} .summer h2{color:rgb(190,120,0)} .autumn{background:rgb(255,245,235);color:rgb(60,35,10)} .autumn h2{color:rgb(180,80,20)} .winter{background:rgb(235,240,255);color:rgb(20,30,55)} .winter h2{color:rgb(80,100,180)}

title: Choose Your Path
description: Use setorigin: links to navigate between pages — click the links in the preview to move between scenes.

origin:
🏚 The Old House

You stand at the gate of an abandoned house. Rain falls softly. The front door is ajar. A path winds around the side.

Go through the front door →Take the side path →
frontDoor:
🚪 The Hallway

Inside it is dark and quiet. A staircase leads up. A door leads toward the kitchen.

Go upstairs →Go to the kitchen →← Back outside
garden:
🌿 The Garden

Overgrown and still. An old well sits at the centre. Something glints at the bottom, just out of reach.

← Return to the gate
upstairs:
🕯 The Upper Landing

A long corridor. Doors on either side, all closed. At the far end, a light flickers under one of them.

← Back downstairs
kitchen:
🍲 The Kitchen

Cold and still. A pot on the stove. Through the cracked window you can see the overgrown garden.

← Back to the hallwayStep into the garden →
grammar._cssStyles: .page{font-family:Georgia,serif;max-width:50ch;padding:1.4rem;line-height:1.8} h2{font-size:1.2rem;margin-bottom:.6rem;color:slateblue} p{margin:.4rem 0;color:rgb(40,40,50)} nav{margin-top:1.2rem;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:.5rem} nav a{color:royalblue;text-decoration:none;font-size:.95rem;padding:.3rem 0;border-bottom:1px solid rgb(220,225,240)} nav a:hover{color:rgb(100,50,200)}

title: Mini Web Page
description: A complete styled page combining header, image, body text and footer.

origin:
#title#

#tagline#

#body#

#body#

#footer#
title: The Wanderer's Log
Deep Sea Notes
Sky Journal
Forest Archive
tagline: Notes from the edge of things.
body: It was the kind of day that makes you want to stop and notice everything around you.
Nobody had been here before — at least, that's what everyone assumed.
The light shifted slowly, turning everything a shade of gold and possibility.
footer: Made by a student, for everyone.

title: Student Example 1
description: A student's work

origin:
🩰#beginning# a generative dance text page!🩰

Designed to inspire your #blahblah#!

With each reload, uncover a new 32 counts of ballet steps that resonates with you. All generated exercises have new ballet steps for you to try! Feel free to select your preferred music and choose your timing.

#lilmessage#!

Heres your personalised #dance#
Start in #danceposition#
#dancelist#

Finish in #danceposition# and bow or curtsey!!!

Bravo!!! Keep refreshing the page to learn more dances created especially for you!!

#gif#For more help visit this link showing you an example of a dancer demonstrating a genrated exercise and the ballet steps included in this webpage :)

learn some key ballet moves or just for some fun
practice some key ballet moves or just for some fun

Good luck, break a leg, and have a blast exploring this generative text webpage!
Best of luck as you dive into this generative text webpage – break a leg and enjoy!
Have fun with this generative text webpage! Best of luck and break a leg!

Enjoy this generative text webpage to the fullest – best of luck and break a leg!
